tasks.title: Persistent RunRepository adapter shipped and verified
tasks.description: Completed Stage 1 / Epic 1 run persistence by replacing bootstrap's default `InMemoryRunRepository` path with a SQLAlchemy-backed durable adapter, persisting full `RunRecord` payloads to `.local/state/runs.sqlite3` by default, exposing `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL`, and adding restart-persistence coverage in the API and application tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the durable run-storage implementation, verified it with `pytest` (`13 passed`), and committed the change as `d6e3041`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly targets research-foundation success criteria for restart-safe runs and reproducible experiment records.

tasks.title: RunRepository deployment database wiring shipped
tasks.description: Completed the deployed-environment RunRepository follow-up by adding the Postgres driver dependency, enabling SQLAlchemy `pool_pre_ping`, pointing Compose `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL` at the `postgres` service with health-gated startup, adding bootstrap coverage for a configured non-SQLite URL, and documenting the Compose/Postgres behavior.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the Postgres deployment wiring, verified API tests (`15 passed`), and committed the change as `aa2b6f4`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closes the deployment-database extension of the research-foundation persistence work without reopening the completed adapter implementation.

tasks.title: Smoke test Compose startup against Postgres-backed run storage
tasks.description: Run an end-to-end Docker Compose startup on a machine with Docker installed to confirm the API waits for Postgres health, boots successfully with `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL` pointed at the `postgres` service, and persists run storage without SQLite fallback.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply called this the only unresolved follow-up from the deployment-database work, but execution still needs a Docker-capable environment outside the current workspace.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Provides the remaining deployment validation for research-foundation persistence without adding new product scope.

tasks.title: Binance-backed candle ingestion and sync status shipped
tasks.description: Completed the Track A / Epic 2 market-data foundation by replacing the default seeded path with a SQL-backed Binance ingestion adapter, persisting OHLCV coverage with freshness reuse, surfacing `sync_status` / lag / gap signals through the API and web UI, and keeping `seeded` only as an offline test fallback.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the Binance-backed ingestion implementation, verified API and web coverage, and committed the change as `74c3a39`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly targets the research-foundation success criterion that market data be Binance-backed with lag visibility and quality checks.

tasks.title: Binance market-data sync now runs as a scheduled background job
tasks.description: Completed the request-path sync removal by moving Binance refresh into a scheduled `MarketDataSyncJob`, wiring startup/shutdown through the FastAPI lifespan, adding sync interval/timeframe configuration, and covering both unsynced request behavior and job lifecycle with API tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the scheduled Binance sync migration, verified it with `pytest` (`27 passed`), and committed the change as `f722373`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the research-foundation market-data lane from request-time freshness checks to predictable ingestion operations suitable for real trading workflows.

tasks.title: Binance historical backfill beyond rolling sync windows shipped
tasks.description: Completed the historical coverage follow-up by teaching Binance background sync to keep the recent window current and then walk backward in batches until a configurable historical candle target is reached, wiring `AKRA_TRADER_MARKET_DATA_HISTORICAL_CANDLE_LIMIT` through bootstrap/config, and adding API/bootstrap coverage for expanding stored history beyond the rolling sync window.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the historical Binance backfill implementation, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`), and committed the change as `b64edf2`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the research-foundation ingestion lane from short-horizon freshness into reproducible historical coverage needed for credible research and replay.

tasks.title: Binance historical backfill target and completion now exposed in ops status
tasks.description: Completed the operator-facing backfill status follow-up by extending the API `InstrumentStatus` shape with historical target, completion ratio, and completion flag fields, populating them for Binance-backed symbols while keeping seeded payloads compatible, and surfacing both summary tiles and per-instrument progress bars in the control-room market-data table.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the backfill visibility work, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `69893ae`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room goal that operators can tell whether Binance data is only recently refreshed or fully backfilled to the configured historical target.

tasks.title: Binance ops now separates backfill count completion from contiguous coverage quality
tasks.description: Completed the ops quality follow-up by splitting Binance instrument status into distinct count-completion and contiguous-quality views, exposing missing-candle-aware quality fields through the API model, surfacing separate control-room summary tiles plus per-instrument Count and Quality columns, and locking in the 'count complete but gap exists' case with API and web verification.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the count-versus-quality split in ops status, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `4da9d13`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Pushes the control-room ops surface from simple backfill progress toward operator-trustworthy data quality signals about whether historical coverage is actually continuous enough to rely on.

tasks.title: Surface exact Binance gap windows alongside backfill quality status
tasks.description: Extend Binance market-data ops status so operators can inspect the exact affected time window of each detected historical gap, not just the aggregate gap count, including API payload fields and control-room presentation that make broken coverage spans directly actionable.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply says the count-versus-quality task is finished and explicitly names exposing the exact affected gap time window as the natural next follow-up, making this the clearest executable successor for the queue head.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room trust surface from coarse quality alarms toward operator-actionable diagnostics by showing where historical Binance coverage actually breaks.

tasks.title: Broaden Binance symbol and timeframe prewarm configuration
tasks.description: Expand configuration beyond the default tracked set so operators can declare additional Binance symbols, timeframes, and startup prewarm coverage targets, with validation and tests that keep ingestion scope explicit.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply named broader symbol/timeframe configuration as a secondary follow-up, but scheduled syncing is the more immediate queue-head task.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Supports the same research-foundation ingestion lane while keeping configurable market coverage separate from the scheduling implementation.

tasks.title: Control-room run lineage panel shipped
tasks.description: Completed the control-room lineage follow-up by surfacing `run.provenance.market_data` inside each run card, including provider, sync status, candle count, timeframe, venue and symbols, requested vs effective windows, last sync time, and lineage issues, with dedicated UI styling for provenance detail.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the control-room lineage UI, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `73edd55`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advances the control-room goal that operator surfaces explain what data a run used, how complete it was, and whether it is safe to trust.

tasks.title: Per-symbol market-data lineage capture shipped for multi-symbol runs
tasks.description: Completed the provenance follow-up by extending run lineage beyond one aggregate `market_data` record so native and delegated multi-symbol runs now persist symbol-scoped lineage in `market_data_by_symbol` while keeping the aggregate field for compatibility, with API/application/freqtrade coverage and serialization tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-symbol lineage implementation, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`24 passed`), and committed the change as `0d8d44b`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds directly on the research-foundation reproducibility work by making dataset provenance precise enough for multi-symbol comparisons and audits.

tasks.title: Per-symbol lineage now surfaced in control-room run details
tasks.description: Completed the control-room follow-up by rendering `provenance.market_data_by_symbol` in run details so multi-symbol runs now expose symbol-scoped sync status, candle counts, effective windows, last sync time, and lineage issues alongside the aggregate lineage block, while older runs gracefully fall back to aggregate-only display.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-symbol lineage control-room surface, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `a71d3a9`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room goal that operators can inspect and trust multi-symbol run provenance from one surface, with backward-compatible fallback for older runs that only retain aggregate lineage.

tasks.title: Add strategy lifecycle and parameter snapshot metadata to the shared run model
tasks.description: Extend the current strategy and run contracts so strategy lifecycle state, version history, parameter snapshots, runtime lane selection, and queryable metadata are explicit across native, reference, and future decision-engine strategies, matching Track B/C and Epic 3 requirements.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest planning answer called out provenance and strategy contract normalization as next-tier foundation work, and current `StrategyMetadata` lacks lifecycle or promotion state even though the ADR and roadmap require it.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Covers the strategy-platform success criteria around explicit runtime lane metadata and comparable version lifecycle.

tasks.title: Keep reference runs explicitly separated with imported provenance
tasks.description: Add the follow-up work needed so NFI or other external-runtime benchmark runs remain visible as reference-lane runs with stored external command, artifact paths, reference identity, and adoption posture rather than looking like native runs in the shared repository model.
tasks.priority_reason: Once durable run storage lands, the next credible benchmark step is to persist imported provenance for external-runtime lanes in line with ADR 0006 and Stage 2 comparability goals.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Supports the reference-lanes success criteria that reference runs stay explicit and traceable rather than becoming hidden native equivalents.
